pattern: A prefix-sum DP over achievable arr1 values at each index counts monotonic splits without enumerating them.
hints: arr2[i] is determined by arr1[i] (arr2[i] = nums[i]-arr1[i]), so you only need to choose the non-decreasing sequence arr1; the non-increasing requirement on arr2 becomes a constraint linking consecutive arr1 choices.

Let dp[i][v] be the number of valid prefixes where arr1[i] = v. Work out which values of arr1[i-1] are compatible with a given arr1[i] = v (arr1[i-1] <= v, and nums[i-1]-arr1[i-1] >= nums[i]-v), then that compatible range is contiguous.

Because the compatible arr1[i-1] range for a fixed arr1[i] = v is always a prefix [0, U], keep a running prefix sum of dp[i-1][*] so each dp[i][v] is an O(1) lookup; sum dp[n-1][*] mod 1e9+7 for the answer.

edge_inputs.description: all values at the minimum, moderate length

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: random walk shape (small steps) to exercise many transitions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(20, 30)]
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        nxt = nums[-1] + rng.randint(-3, 3)
        nxt = max(1, min(50, nxt))
        nums.append(nxt)
    return [nums]
